core_mechanism: A driven (totally asymmetric) exclusion process of hard-core particles biased-hopping in one direction along a one-dimensional lattice, where each particle advances through an internal multi-state stochastic cycle: the single-particle limit is a continuous-time Markov cycle whose first-passage statistics yield exact dwell-time distributions, and the many-particle case with mutual exclusion produces boundary-controlled nonequilibrium phase transitions mapped as a phase diagram.
brief_reason: The abstract models each unit as a biased stochastic stepper with an internal mechanochemical cycle (alternating translocation and pause) giving an exact dwell-time distribution, then adds steric exclusion of many units on one track to obtain a phase diagram in the space of control parameters, which is exactly a driven one-dimensional exclusion process with internal states.
